content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 9S814-2329 
J. J. Jelincic 
3828 Hillcrest Lane 
Sacramento, CA 95821 
RE: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 11-033 
Dear Mr. Jelincic: 
(916) 322-S660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
April 6, 2011 
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the statement of economic 
interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
You have asked several questions regarding the reporting of interests in the state's 
Savings Plus Program. the Schwab Personal Choice Retirement Account offered under the 
Savings Plus Program and exchange traded funds. 
The Commission is reviewing the disclosure and conflict-of-interest rules relating to 
investments. We anticipate the Commission will consider these issues in the near future. Until 
then, you need not report the investments described in your letter. 
Sincerely. 
John W. Wallace 
Assistant 7 Counsel 
--c/, cic	 
By: 
Valentina Joyce 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8 IOOO through 9 IOl4. All statutory 
references are 10 the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regula1iuns. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question: You have asked several questions regarding the reporting of interests in the state's Savings Plus Program, the Schwab Personal Choice Retirement Account offered under the Savings Plus Program and exchange traded funds.
sections.conclusion: The Commission is reviewing the disclosure and conflict-of-interest rules relating to investments. We anticipate the Commission will consider these issues in the near future. Until then, you need not report the investments described in your letter.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether certain investments in the Savings Plus Program, Schwab Personal Choice Retirement Account, and exchange traded funds must be reported under the statement of economic interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the investments described do not need to be reported at this time, pending the Commission's ongoing review of disclosure and conflict-of-interest rules relating to investments.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains question and conclusion content in letter body. File number 11-033W indicates a withdrawal; however, the document is substantive advice rather than a withdrawal notice.

QUESTION: You have asked several questions regarding the reporting of interests in the state's Savings Plus Program, the Schwab Personal Choice Retirement Account offered under the Savings Plus Program and exchange traded funds.

CONCLUSION: The Commission is reviewing the disclosure and conflict-of-interest rules relating to investments. We anticipate the Commission will consider these issues in the near future. Until then, you need not report the investments described in your letter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to J. J. Jelincic's request for advice regarding whether certain investments in the Savings Plus Program and related accounts must be disclosed under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC determined that the investments need not be reported pending completion of its review of investment disclosure rules.
